title: petastoma phaseoloides
intro: Petastoma phaseoloides, also known as Creeping Jenny or Creeping Spurge, is a flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. It is a perennial herb used in herbal medicine, particularly for its medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The plant is known to provide health benefits including relief from skin conditions, fever reduction, and support for digestive issues. Traditionally, Creeping Jenny is prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it has also been used in topical applications for wound care and skin treatments.
